fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: High growth potential from small companies; Bandhan has shown consistent returns with better risk control than peers

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund-Direct Plan-Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Strong long-term track record in mid-sized companies; complements small-cap exposure

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Low-cost way to own 150 midcaps; reduces active fund risk and keeps fees low

fund.name: Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Provides global exposure to top US companies; diversifies away from Indian market risks

fund.name: Mirae Asset Small Cap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: Mirae Asset Investment Managers (India) Pvt. Ltd

allocations.rationale: Adds a second strong small-cap manager for diversification; Mirae has a proven strategy

fund.name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: Large, stable fund that can handle big money; spreads risk across market sizes

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera.
Step 2: Transfer ₹50 lakh from your bank to the investment app.
Step 3: Invest ₹5 lakh as lumpsum in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund.
Step 4: Start monthly SIPs: ₹80,000 in Bandhan Small Cap, ₹40,000 in Motilal Oswal Midcap, ₹20,000 each in Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150, Motilal Oswal S&P 500, and Mirae Small Cap.
Step 5: Set auto-pay from your bank account to ensure SIPs run smoothly.
review_checkpoints: After 3 months: Confirm all SIPs are running without fail.
After 1 year: Check if any fund underperformed its category by more than 3% over 1 year.
Year 3, 5, 10: Major review of portfolio progress toward 15Cr goal.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance once every year. If any category (small, mid, flexi, international) grows to more than 10 percentage points above its target, shift the excess to underweight categories. Example: If small-cap grows from 50% to 60% of portfolio, move 5-6% back to index or flexi funds.
plan.rationale: You're 30, single, and earn well — this is the perfect time to take aggressive bets. Over 25 years, even slight return differences create massive wealth. We've put 70% in small and mid caps to chase alpha, added a low-cost index fund to keep fees down, and included US stocks for global diversification. The lumpsum in HDFC Flexi Cap gives you instant broad market exposure with a proven fund. At ₹2 lakh/month SIP, you're investing seriously — this plan is built to aim for your ₹15 crore FIRE goal.
risks: Small and mid-cap funds can fall 40-50% in a bad year — don't panic and exit.
US market performs differently than India; S&P 500 may not always rise when Indian markets do.
High monthly SIP means missing payments can hurt compounding — automate it.
Inflation or lifestyle creep could reduce surplus — keep investing discipline strong.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: Approaching startup funding goal — reduce risk

aps.reasoning: passive_instrument_fraction: 20% of total portfolio is in index funds (Midcap 150 10%, S&P 500 10%). Within the 100% equity sleeve, this equals 20%. Score ≈ 0.21. turnover_score: Annual rebalancing is prescribed; no monthly/quarterly shifts, but there is an under-performance review every year plus step-up SIP. Mid-range → 0.6. cost_emphasis_score: Only one sentence cites low fees; 70% of money is placed in high-cost active funds (expense 1–1.2%). Cost minimisation is secondary → 0.3. research_vs_cost_score: Rationale stresses manager track-records, alpha potential, fund selection; no mention of broad-market beta → 0.0. time_horizon_alignment_score: 25-year goal, no tactical windows; only infrequent reviews → 0.9. portfolio_activeness_score: Categories are Small-cap (50%), Mid-cap active (20%), Flexi-cap (10%) – all high-conviction active bets; only 20% passive. Missing risk metrics but category alone signals high activeness → 0.15.

pqs.reasoning: The plan aggressively pursues the investor’s FIRE-cum-startup goal with 100 % equity and a 10 %-step-up SIP, matching both horizon and risk appetite. It over-weights small/mid-cap (70 %) and adds low-cost index and US exposure, exactly the alpha the profile asks for. Diversification across five managers and geographies is good, though 20 % of NAV comes from the same fund house (Motilal Oswal); still no asset-class or single-fund concentration. Return assumptions (14–18 %) are stretched but explicitly flagged as aggressive and tempered by bear-case 10 %. Rebalancing rules and glide-path (de-risk 10 and 20 years out) are internally consistent with the stated goals. Tax efficiency is strong: every fund chosen enjoys equity taxation (≥65 % equity), correctly omitting ELSS because the investor is on the new regime; S&P 500 index also receives favourable treatment under India’s revised tax code.
